title: John Swinney seeks meeting with UK Government over Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: JOHN Swinney is seeking a meeting with the UK Government over the US military's use of Prestwick Airport. It comes as flight tracking data analysed by the BBC showed that some of the American military planes which have passed through the Ayrshire airport in recent weeks have been directly involved in supporting the US-Israeli strikes against Iran. The First Minister has asked for clarity that US planes were not part of offensive operations, and is also seeking for a meeting with the Cabinet Office to discuss the matter, a Government spokesperson said.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: John Swinney at First Minister's Questions on Thursday (Image: Jane Barlow) Swinney previously said he would "consider" banning US military aircraft from using the airport, which is owned by the Scottish Government , if it was confirmed they were involved in offensive strikes on Iran. In an interview on the BBC's Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg programme over the weekend, he said that he is seeking clarity from the UK Government about the role of US planes using Prestwick Airport.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: READ MORE: How archaeologists digging in a back garden near Glasgow found a historic fort A row has since erupted over whether the Scottish Government actually has the authority to ban the US military from Scottish airports.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – John Swinney seeks meeting with UK Government over US military using Prestwick
